title: [FIRRTL] infer width (mux)
body:
The following FIRRTL program:

```python
circuit top_mod :
  module top_mod :
    input sel: UInt
    output tmp116: UInt<8>
    tmp116 <= mux(sel, UInt<8>(24), UInt<8>(42))
```

Compiled with `firtool a_top_mod.fir --lower-to-hw --expand-whens --infer-widths --lowering-options=noAlwaysFF --verilog -o=a_top_mod_new.v` fails with the following error:

```
a_top_mod.fir:5:15: error: 'firrtl.mux' op operand #0 must be UInt<1>, but got '!firrtl.uint'
    tmp116 <= mux(sel, UInt<8>(24), UInt<8>(42))
              ^
a_top_mod.fir:5:15: note: see current operation: %0 = "firrtl.mux"(%sel, %c24_ui8, %c42_ui8) : (!firrtl.uint, !firrtl.uint<8>, !firrtl.uint<8>) -> !firrtl.uint<8>
```

Compiled with `firrtl-1.5-SNAPSHOT` produces this Verilog:

```verilog
module top_mod(
  input        sel,
  output [7:0] tmp116
);
  assign tmp116 = sel ? 8'h18 : 8'h2a;
endmodule
```
